title: carphalea cloiselii
intro: Carphalea cloiselii, also known as the Madagascar carphalea, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the hibiscus family (Malvaceae). Traditionally, Carphalea cloiselii has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating various skin conditions. Carphalea cloiselii can be prepared as a herbal infusion or decoction, and its extracts have been studied for their potential in developing new medicines, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
